Cultivated worldwide, particularly in the tropics and subtropics, as an ornamental plant or living fence. Naturalized in thickets and secondary forests. Naturalized.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 694 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 323; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 39; Borssum, Blumea 14 (1966) 29; Romeroso et al., Asian J. Biol. Michigan Herb. 23 (2001) 258 ; Cañeda et al., Davao Research J. 16 (2025) 91. ssp. tiliaceus. Distribution: PALAWAN. Notes: World tropics and subtropics. Throughout the Philippines, particularly along seashores and tidal streams. Sometimes further inland following riverbanks, or intentionally planted as hedge or ornamental. Seeds capable of long-distance dispersal by oceanic currents. 0m. Native. ssp. crestaensis Borss.Waalk., Blumea 14 (1966) 36; ≡ Talipariti crestaense (Borss.Waalk.) Fryxell, Contr. Univ. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 695 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 322; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 38; Borssum, Blumea 14 (1966) 64.
species.notes: Origin not known. Cultivated in all tropical regions, mostly as vegetable and for fiber.

species.citation: (Cav.) R.L.Barrett & M.M.Hanes , Austral. Syst. Bot. 38 (2025) SB24013: 70; ≡ Hibiscus radiatus Cav. , Diss. 3, Tertia Diss. Bot. (1787) 150 ; Fern.-Vill., Novis. App. (1880) 24; Borssum, Blumea 14 (1966) 60. ≠ Hibiscus cannabinus auct. non L. ; Merr., PJS 3 c (1908) Bot. 244; EPFP 3 (1923) 37.
species.distribution: India, Myanmar. LUZON: Mountain Province, NCR, Pangasinan.
species.notes: Widely grown as a vegetable or medicinal in Malesia and escaping cultivation.

species.citation: (L.) M.M.Hanes & R.L.Barrett , Austral. Syst. Bot. 38 (2025) SB24013: 79; ≡ Hibiscus surattensis L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 696 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 38; Borssum, Blumea 14 (1966) 57; Type: from India.
species.distribution: Africa, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam. species.notes: Early secondary growths and waste places near human settlements in the lowlands. (?-)1200m.

species.citation: Burm.f. , Fl. Ind. (N. L. Burman) (1768) 147 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 318; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 33; Borssum, Blumea 14 (1966) 186; Type: from Java. ssp. acuta. (1782) 307, nom. illeg., non Mill. Distribution: Africa, America, Pacific Ocean. LUZON: Mountain Province. Notes: Naturalized in Sumatra and Philippines. Naturalized.
species.distribution: Africa, America, Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Cambodia, Caribbean, China, India, Indian Ocean, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, Nicobar Isls, Ryukyu Isls, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Mountain Province, MINDORO, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines. Likely not native to the Philippines (Merrill 1912, Van Borssum Waalkes 1966). Road sides, waste places.

species.citation: L. , Syst. Nat., 10th Ed. 2 (1759) 1145 ; Borssum, Blumea 14 (1966) 179. = Sida longistipula Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 30 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 34.
species.distribution: America. LUZON: Ilocos Norte.
species.notes: Introduced in Philippines. Open places.

species.citation: (Burm.f.) Borss.Waalk. , Blumea 14 (1966) 182; ≡ Melochia cordata Burm.f. , Fl. Ind. (N. L. Burman) (1768) 143. = Sida veronicifolia Lam. , Encycl. (MA, neo), Luzon: Port Cavite.
species.distribution: Pantropic. LUZON.
species.notes: Pantropical species of unknown origin. Not native in Malesia. Open grasslands at sea level (Merrill 1923).

species.notes: Throughout the Philippines; likely not native (Merrill 1912). Open waste places.

species.citation: (L.) Sol. ex Corrêa , Ann. Mus. Natl. Hist. Nat. 9 (1807) 290 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 324; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 42; Borssum, Blumea 14 (1966) 106; Fosb. & Sachet, Smithsonian Contr. Bot. No. 7 (1972) 8; ≡ Hibiscus populneus L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 694.
species.distribution: Pantropic. APULIT, JOLO, LOGBON, LUZON: Aurora (photos), Batangas, Bulacan, Camarines Sur, La Union, NCR, Quezon, Rizal, MINASAWA, MINDANAO: Davao, Davao Oriental (photos), Davao del Sur, MINDORO, PALAUI, PALAWAN, PANAY, PAPAHAG, TABLAS, TAWI-TAWI.
species.notes: Widespread throughout the Philippines along seashores. Occasionally found a few km inland, whether intentionally planted or not. 0-5m (photos).

species.citation: (Roxb.) Kostel. , Allg. Med.-Pharm. Fl. 5 (1836) 1861 ; Fosb. & Sachet, Smithsonian Contr. Bot. No. 7 (1972) 10; Aragones et al., Bot. ident. Handb. Philip. Mangrove Trees (1998) 57; ≡ Hibiscus populneoides Roxb. , Fl. Ind. (Roxburgh) 3 (1832) 191.

species.notes: Throughout Malesia; cultivated in some parts of the neotropics.
